rulings.Calendar Matter: APPLICATION AND HEARING FOR RIGHT TO ATTACH ORDER
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for July 1, 2021 line 4. PETITIONER 345 CALIFORNIA L.P. APPLICATION AND HEARING FOR RIGHT TO ATTACH ORDER is GRANTED in the amount of $364,573.20. No opposition filed. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: STEVE RYAN VS. MITCHELL ROSENFELD ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for July 1, 2021 line 3. PLAINTIFF STEVE RYAN Notice Of Motion For Reconsideration is DENIED. On January 21, 2021 Plaintiff filed a Motion to Vacate Judgement. The Judgement was entered based on an Order granting Defendants' Motion for Summary Judgment on July 24, 2020. While Plaintiff admitted that he knew about the pending motion for summary judgment (January 21, 2021 Declaration, paragraph 7), Plaintiff believed that due to COVID-19 he would be receiving a separate notice of the new hearing date. Plaintiff did not explain why Plaintiff did not check the register of actions available on-line to determine the new date or why he did not file an opposition in accordance with the original hearing as no factual support was provided for Plaintiff's belief that the Court simply closed for business due to COVID. Plaintiff did not submit a proposed opposition to the Motion for Summary Judgment and did not provide any evidence explaining the delay in bringing the motion to vacate summary judgment order from July 29, 2020 (See POS for the Order Granting Motion for Summary Judgment) to January 21, 2021. Nevertheless the Court gave Plaintiff the benefit of the doubt regarding confusion surrounding COVID and new hearing date for the Motion for Summary Judgment; the Court provided Plaintiff an opportunity to fix the defects in the Motion and allowed Plaintiff until April 2, 2021 to file (a) proposed opposition and (b) medical evidence of the flare-ups of pre-existing conditions that caused an over 6-month delay in seeking relief. By April 2, 2021 deadline Plaintiff filed an incomplete opposition that did not include a response to the separate statement (to the extent Plaintiff may now be claiming that Separate Statement should not be responded to unless it is served electronically, Plaintiff appears to misread CRC 3.1350(i) which requires the moving party to provide an electronic copy "on request" - no evidence is provided that such request was made prior to April 2, 2021 and no authority is cited for the proposition that failure to provide an electronic copy excuses responding party's obligations). Plaintiff did not attempt to provide any medical evidence by April 2, 2021 or before the hearing on April 23, 2021. On April 16, 2021 Plaintiff attempted to file a 3-page unsigned declaration, which was not a declaration from a medical provider and did not contain any documents from a medical provider. With this motion Plaintiff provides records dating back to October 14, 2014. The same records that Plaintiff had in his possession since at least December 22, 2014 when they were submitted to the Court in action CGC-10-504983. These records shed no light on Plaintiff's purported inability to file a Motion to Vacate between August 2020 and January 2021. Therefore, there are no new facts, law or circumstances to warrant reconsideration of the Motion to Vacate. Even if the Motion for Reconsideration were to be granted, based on the evidence currently before the Court the Motion to Vacate would be denied again. =(501/CFH) part 1 of 2

rulings.Case Title: JODI HILLMAN VS. JOHN ABELSON ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Alternative Summary Adjudication
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for July 1, 2021 line 1. DEFENDANT JOHN ABELSON, CHRISTINE GUTHRIE MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Alternative Summary Adjudication is DENIED. Moving party failed to carry its burden and show that there are no triable issues of material fact. E.g. one of the material facts included in the separate statement for MSJ and each issue for MSA is: "Plaintiff began her tenancy at the subject property in 2000," defendant appears to be disputing the existence of tenancy. See response to Plaintiff's Separate Statement, "Defendants deny any implication suggesting Plaintiff was a tenant." A landlord-tenant relationship is asserted at the basis of each cause of action in this action and the existence of such relationships is both (1) included as a material fact by the litigants; and (2) disputed. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: GANE SUGALI VS. OCEAN AVENUE REAL ESTATE FUND, LLC, ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for July 1, 2021 line 2. DEFENDANT TONY GARNICKI, OCEAN AVENUE REAL ESTATE FUND, LLC DEMURRER to 2ND Amended COMPLAINT is SUSTAINED in part and overruled in part. Demurrer to the fraud cause of action is sustained with final leave to amend for Plaintiff to allege facts, not conclusions in support of each element of each cause of action as to each defendant. Plaintiff to allege as to each specific representation - the representation, to whom by whom, how and when it was made; that representation was untrue at the time it was made; that Plaintiff reasonably relied on such representation; and that Plaintiff was damaged. All amendments to be on bold font. General statements and conclusions are not enough, assertions like "successful flipper," "safe investment," and "quick return" are not sufficient. The Court also notes that the facts of the complaint appear to indicate that Plaintiff was a lender, however Plaintiff keeps referring to an "investment" and comparing this loan "past investors." If Plaintiff uses the terms "lender" and "investor" interchangeably, Plaintiff should pick just one term to avoid the confusion, especially as it relates to reasonable reliance on representation regarding repayments of loans as compared to payments made to past investors. Demurrer to the cause of action for breach of contract is overruled. Moving party fails to cite any authority for the proposition that (1) a demurrer may be sustained only to a part of cause of action, i.e. there is no basis for demurrer to the breach of contact regarding the principal on the loan; and (2) usury interest may properly be addressed on demurrer, i.e. none of the cases cited were resolved on a demurrer. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.
